article.title: FDA Grants Accelerated Approval to BeOne's Sonrotoclax as First BCL2 Inhibitor Indicated for Relapsed or Refractory Mantle Cell Lymphoma
article.summary: Beqalzi (sonrotoclax) clears U.S. review with a 52% ORR and 15.8-month median duration of response in post-BTK MCL patients, opening a new drug class.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has granted accelerated approval to BeOne Medicines' sonrotoclax, branded Beqalzi, for the treatment of adult patients with relapsed or refractory mantle cell lymphoma (MCL) who have received at least two prior lines of systemic therapy including a Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitor, [BeOne announced](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma). The company describes the clearance as making Beqalzi the first and only BCL2 inhibitor approved in MCL and the first new BCL2 inhibitor approved in a decade in the United States, [per the announcement](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma). [BioSpace's news coverage](https://www.biospace.com/fda/beone-wins-mantle-cell-lymphoma-approval-opening-new-therapy-class) framed the clearance as opening a new therapy class for the disease.

## What We Know

### Trial design and efficacy

The approval is supported by the Phase 1/2 BGB-11417-201 study (NCT05471843), an [efficacy and safety dataset](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma) that included an independent review of efficacy data. According to BeOne, the overall response rate was 52% (95% CI, 42-62), with a complete response rate of 16% (95% CI, 9.1-24.0) and a median time to response of 1.9 months. The median duration of response was 15.8 months (95% CI, 7.4 months-NE) after a median response follow-up of 11.9 months, [the company reported](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma).

### Safety profile

The most common adverse reactions occurring in at least 15 percent of patients were pneumonia (16%) and fatigue (16%), [BeOne said](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma). Laboratory abnormalities included decreases in lymphocytes (29%) and neutrophils (18%). Serious infections occurred in 14% of patients, with the most common Grade 3 or greater infection being pneumonia (10%), and laboratory or clinical tumor lysis syndrome was reported in 7% of patients, [per the company](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma).

### Regulatory designations

The FDA granted sonrotoclax Breakthrough Therapy Designation, Fast Track Designation and Orphan Drug Designation for the MCL indication, [BeOne said](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma).

### What clinicians and the company said

Michael Wang, M.D., Global Principal Investigator and Puddin Clarke Endowed Professor in the Department of Lymphoma and Myeloma at The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center, said in BeOne's announcement that "the data supporting the approval of sonrotoclax in the U.S. confirm its role as a foundational therapy for mantle cell lymphoma in the post-BTK inhibitor setting, and demonstrate that it can deliver robust disease control when treatment choices are limited and outcomes are poor," [the company stated](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma).

Amit Agarwal, M.D., Ph.D., Chief Medical Officer for Hematology at BeOne Medicines, said that "today's approval of BEQALZI represents critical progress for patients with mantle cell lymphoma and reinforces our strategy of building foundational medicines designed to raise the standard of care in B-cell malignancies," [according to the same release](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma).

Meghan Gutierrez, Chief Executive Officer of the Lymphoma Research Foundation, added that the FDA approval "represents significant progress for the U.S. mantle cell lymphoma community, offering renewed hope for patients and families who have exhausted other available therapies," [BeOne reported](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma).

### Disease context

Approximately 3,300 new cases of MCL are diagnosed in the United States each year, [BeOne noted](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma), making it one of the rarer non-Hodgkin lymphoma subtypes. The company also said more than 2,200 patients have been enrolled across the [broad sonrotoclax global development program](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma), which spans additional B-cell malignancies. Beqalzi is also approved in China for relapsed or refractory MCL and for previously treated chronic lymphocytic leukemia and small lymphocytic lymphoma, [per the company](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/beone-medicines-beqalzi-sonrotoclax-approved-by-u-s-fda-as-first-and-only-bcl2-inhibitor-for-r-r-mantle-cell-lymphoma).

### Market positioning

[BioSpace](https://www.biospace.com/fda/beone-wins-mantle-cell-lymphoma-approval-opening-new-therapy-class) reported that Truist Securities analysts described the approval as "incremental," citing the "relatively small" market for relapsed or refractory MCL and forecasting between $300 million and $400 million in sales for Beqalzi in this indication.

## What We Don't Know

Because the clearance is an accelerated approval, continued marketing authorization is contingent on confirmatory clinical-benefit data from later-stage studies. Durable overall-survival benefit in this MCL setting has not yet been demonstrated in a randomized confirmatory trial described in the materials reviewed here. The pivotal BGB-11417-201 study is single-arm, with no direct head-to-head comparison against AbbVie and Roche's venetoclax, which has been used off-label in MCL.

## Analysis

Mantle cell lymphoma has been a sequencing problem rather than a first-line one. Patients who progress through a BTK inhibitor have had limited targeted options, and BCL2 inhibition — long established in chronic lymphocytic leukemia through venetoclax — has been available in MCL only off-label. A 52 percent response rate with a median duration of response approaching 16 months in a post-BTK population is not a curative result, but it is the first time the FDA has formally endorsed a BCL2 inhibitor for this disease, which is what BeOne is highlighting. The narrow indication and modest analyst forecast for the standalone label suggest the immediate commercial impact will be measured; the longer-term question is whether sonrotoclax can establish BeOne as a durable presence across B-cell malignancies beyond chronic lymphocytic leukemia.
